raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2491 will ban the sale of anti-aging cosmetic products to kids under the age of 13.",
    "Social media platforms like TikTok and Instagram have influenced children to use anti-aging products.",
    "Common ingredients in anti-aging skin products, such as retinols and glycolic acid, can cause skin irritations.",
    "Anti-aging products designed for adults are not suitable for children and preteens.",
    "Children and teens using anti-aging products may experience adverse effects like dryness, peeling, and potential dermatitis.",
    "Babies and young children have a faster skin cell turnover rate than adults.",
    "Children's skin is more sensitive than adults' skin due to a thinner epidermis and weaker skin barrier.",
    "Dermatologists report observing adverse effects in young patients using products too harsh for their sensitive skin.",
    "Using anti-aging skincare products can lead to psychological impacts related to self-esteem and mental health in children.",
    "The British Association of Dermatologists has raised concerns that children using anti-aging products may develop irreversible skin problems."
  ]
}
```
